dialogue.utterance: Dispatch Unit 3 to an apartment in [CITY] for a 911 call concerning breathing problems in a 72-year-old male.
Hello sir, my name is Alex. Can you tell me why you called 911?
I've had trouble breathing for several days, and my inhaler isn't helping like it used to.
Mr. Smith, can you tell me your name and where we are?
I'm George Smith, and I'm at my apartment in [CITY].
I'm going to shine a light in your eyes. It's okay.
Pupils appear equal and reactive with light.
Is there anything you're feeling in your throat that feels blocked?
Airway appears clear and patent with no signs of obstruction.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Rate and depth of respirations
dialogue.utterance: Your breathing is rapid and heavy-28 breaths per minute.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Presence of adventitious breath sounds
dialogue.utterance: I detect bilateral inspiratory wheezes during breathing.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Oxygen saturation measured with pulse oximetry
dialogue.utterance: Your pulse oximeter shows 100% saturation without supplemental oxygen.

dialogue.micro_intent: check circulation - Pulse rate and quality
dialogue.utterance: Let me feel your pulse here on your wrist.
Your skin looks normal in color, smooth, and warm to the touch.
Your blood pressure is recorded at 165/75.
Mr. Smith, what year is it, and are you able to tell me what you plan to eat for dinner tonight?
2020…no, I don't have that clear. I'm just too breathless to think.
I need to perform a quick fingerstick for blood sugar.
The blood glucose test returned a result of 0, though we don't see signs of low sugar.
Have you experienced any other symptoms or noticed any swelling recently?
I have significant swelling in my legs and genital area-urination is difficult due to firm swelling.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: How long has this issue been occurring?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: A few days. It's gotten worse with more shortness of breath and swelling.
On a scale from 0 to 10, how breathless are you feeling right now?
Probably around a 7.
And do you experience any chest, abdominal, or other pain?
No pain-just the trouble breathing and swelling.
When you say swelling, is it only in your legs, or is it on other parts of your body too?
Both legs and around the genital area. It's been tough to pull back the skin to urinate.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Just one more quick examination of your pupils.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Pupils are still equal and reactive; no unexpected findings.
I'll listen to your lungs once more.
Breath sounds continue to be diminished bilaterally with inspiratory wheezes.
Your respiratory rate remains at 28 with labored breathing.
Still hearing significant wheezing on both sides.
Heart rate is consistent at 78 and steady.
Blood pressure continues at 165/75.
Oxygen saturation still reads 100 percent.
EKG display reveals sinus rhythm on the 4-lead monitor.
We are placing on four-lead EKG monitoring to get a baseline.
ECG is stable and has good signal.
Let us know if your breathing worsens during this time.
I am going to administer a nebulizer treatment as we speak.
Giving you Albuterol 5 mg and Atrovent 0.5 mg in the nebulizer now.
Just take slow, deep breaths in and out through the nebulizer.
Mr. Smith, have you noticed any improvement since using the nebulizer?
Yes, it's a bit easier now.
Your breathing rate has decreased to 24. You appear to be breathing more easily.
Heart rate remains steady at 76 and regular.
Blood pressure is now 140/79.
We will also apply oxygen at 6 liters per minute for extra support.
We are adhering to protocol 3-11 for respiratory distress, possibly a COPD flare.
Mr. Smith, how are you feeling now?
Still struggling a little, but definitely better. That swelling still isn't going down.
Pulse rate is stable at 76 and regular.
Blood pressure is 140/79 without any changes.
Mr. Smith, are you ready to head to the hospital, such as [COMPANY_NAME]?
Yes, I'd like to go to [COMPANY_NAME].
Let's lay you flat for comfort. I'll help you down.
Securing you now with straps to ensure smooth transport.
Mr. Smith has active COPD with lower body swelling noted and partial improvement following nebulizer use.
All vital signs are within acceptable range, with no reported pain and a pain scale of zero.
Full documentation is in the EPCR. He signed the form himself to grant permission.
